expected_curve: naive/value_profile advantage monotone increasing in ACCUM_THRESHOLD (I2S arms starve harder as more distinct tokens are required)

acceptance.rule: I2S-lacking arms (naive, value_profile) >= I2S-having arms (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) at every dose; gap widens with ACCUM_THRESHOLD: ~parity at ACCUM_THRESHOLD=2, naive/vp > cmp/vpc at 32, naive/vp >> cmp/vpc (cmp/vpc near 0) at 96. CONSERVATIVE: stratification need only hold at the high end; flat/inconclusive at low end is acceptable and honest.

design_basis: The gate fires only when the runtime distinct-token accumulator exceeds ACCUM_THRESHOLD, requiring DIVERSITY (many distinct token values), which no single I2S literal substitution can supply. An early exact keyword CMP attracts I2SRandReplace, anchoring cmplog/vpc corpora on the canonical literal and re-homogenizing the token region; the higher ACCUM_THRESHOLD, the more distinct tokens needed and the more the anchored I2S corpus starves relative to diversity-by-havoc.
notes: ACCUM_THRESHOLD=2: trivial diversity bar; all four fuzzers expected to crash readily -> near parity (baseline that the harness is reachable).
ACCUM_THRESHOLD=8: mirrors the libxml2 MAX_HASH_LEN/MAX_FREE_NODES regime; modest separation expected as I2S anchoring begins to bite.
ACCUM_THRESHOLD=32: naive/value_profile should clear it via havoc diversity; cmplog/vpc lag as I2SRandReplace keeps collapsing the token region toward the keyword literal.

ACCUM_THRESHOLD=96: needs >96 distinct byte values in the ~247-byte token region -- reachable in principle but a steep diversity bar; expected to favor naive/value_profile heavily while cmplog/vpc are near 0. If even naive cannot clear it within budget, read the high anchor as a difficulty ceiling rather than I2S-specific failure (adjudicator may treat as inconclusive-by-ceiling).

Anti-family caveat: 'anchoring' is a corpus/scheduler dynamic, not reproducible within one execution; the structural trap (attractor CMP + diversity-only gate) is what is isolated. An inconclusive verdict is an honest outcome.
